steps.title: Track what the text does
steps.content:
The text states a conclusion from Hanel's study (people are more polite if they reflect on values first), then describes the method: how participants were split, what each group wrote about, and what was observed.

So the text both states the finding and walks through how it was reached.

steps.title: Match the purpose
steps.content:
C captures both halves: explain the study's conclusion and how the team arrived at it.

A claims the researchers were surprised (not stated). B suggests improving the design (not the focus). D claims a widely held belief (no such belief is described).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
